name: Quadraphonic Mixer
description: This algorithm is based on the disting EX algorithm of the same name. It is a quadraphonic mixer – that is, it has four outputs for four speakers, rather than the usual two for a stereo mixer. The algorithm provides two means to perform that classic quadraphonic trick, spinning the audio around the audience. Each channel has a Spinner and an Orbiter. By default, each channel uses its own, but you can set it up to share spinners and orbiters between channels. The spinner internally modulates the Angle parameter; it only works if the channel is using polar coordinates. Therefore it spins the position around the centre. The orbiter is what was called the spinner in the disting EX version. It spins the audio position around the nominal position set by the coordinates; it therefore works in both polar and rectangular coordinate modes.
gui_description: When on the common parameters page, the GUI shows a mixer view similar to that of the Mixer Stereo algorithm, though with four outputs. When viewing the parameters of a channel, a graphical view is shown representing the channel position in the quadraphonic field.

output_ports.name: Front left output
output_ports.description: The bus to use for the front left speaker output.

output_ports.description: The bus to use for the front left right output.
